question: What is the primary cause of battery overheating in the C-phone series?
correct_answer: The primary cause is a firmware mismatch in the power management unit (PMU) affecting the charging cycle regulation.
support: The C-phone series uses a high-density lithium-polymer battery. Investigations reveal that the firmware version 2.4.1 incorrectly interprets voltage spikes during rapid charging, causing the PMU to keep the charging gate open longer than necessary. This leads to thermal runaway. The fix involves a mandatory over-the-air (OTA) update to version 2.4.2.

question: How can I fix the 'Camera Failed' error on the C-phone X1?
correct_answer: Clear the camera cache and restart. If persistent, check for the latest OS patch which resolves the sensor initialization timeout.
support: The 'Camera Failed' error typically stems from a race condition during the boot process where the camera sensor initializes before the image signal processor (ISP) is ready. Clearing the cache forces a re-initialization. However, the permanent fix is in the OS patch released last month, which adds a 200ms delay to the sensor startup sequence.

question: Does the C-phone support 5G millimeter wave bands?
correct_answer: Yes, but only on the Pro and Ultra models sold in supported regions.
support: The standard C-phone model is equipped with a sub-6GHz 5G modem for broad coverage. The Pro and Ultra models include additional antenna modules required for mmWave frequencies (24GHz and above), which offer higher speeds but have limited range. Customers should check their specific model number against the regional support list.

question: Why is my C-phone screen flickering at low brightness?
correct_answer: This is likely due to the Pulse Width Modulation (PWM) dimming frequency being too low for some sensitive eyes; a software update has added a DC Dimming option.
support: OLED screens often use PWM to control brightness, turning pixels on and off rapidly. At low brightness, the 'off' periods are longer, which some users perceive as flickering. Company C released a 'Comfort View' update that enables DC Dimming, adjusting voltage instead of cycling power, to eliminate this flicker.

question: What is the maximum charging speed of the C-phone 15?
correct_answer: The C-phone 15 supports up to 45W wired charging and 15W wireless charging.
support: To achieve 45W speeds, users must use a charger compatible with the USB-PD 3.0 (PPS) standard. Using a standard 5G charger will limit speeds to 15-25W. The phone's thermal management system will also throttle charging speeds if the device temperature exceeds 38°C.

question: Can I use my old C-phone charger with the new model?
correct_answer: Yes, if it is a USB-C charger, but charging speeds may be limited compared to the recommended 45W adapter.
support: The new C-phone series complies with standard USB Power Delivery protocols. An older 18W charger will safely charge the phone, but it will take significantly longer (approx. 2 hours vs 45 mins) to reach full capacity.

question: How do I enable the 'AI Eraser' feature in the photo gallery?
correct_answer: Open a photo, tap 'Edit', select 'AI Tools', and choose 'Eraser'. Requires an internet connection for processing complex scenes.
support: The AI Eraser uses a cloud-based diffusion model to fill in the background. While simple removals happen on-device (NPU), complex textures require the image to be briefly uploaded to Company C's secure inference server. Ensure you are logged into your Company C account.

question: Is the C-phone waterproof?
correct_answer: It is water-resistant with an IP68 rating, meaning it can withstand submersion in 1.5 meters of freshwater for up to 30 minutes.
support: IP68 does not mean 'waterproof' indefinitely. Resistance decreases with wear and tear. Saltwater, chlorinated water, and high-pressure streams (like showers) can compromise the seals. Warranty does not cover liquid damage if the moisture indicator is tripped.

question: Why does Bluetooth audio lag when gaming on the C-phone?
correct_answer: Bluetooth latency is inherent, but enabling 'Game Mode' in settings forces the use of the low-latency aptX Adaptive codec.
support: Standard Bluetooth audio has a latency of ~200ms. 'Game Mode' negotiates a lower buffer size and prioritizes audio packets over background data, reducing latency to ~60ms, provided the headset also supports low-latency codecs.

question: How can I recover deleted photos on the C-phone?
correct_answer: Check the 'Recently Deleted' folder in the Gallery app, where photos are kept for 30 days before permanent deletion.
support: The filesystem does not immediately overwrite the data blocks. The 'Recently Deleted' folder is a logical container. If 30 days have passed, data recovery is difficult without specialized forensic software, as the NAND flash controller may have performed garbage collection (TRIM).

question: What does the 'Optimized Charging' feature do?
correct_answer: It learns your daily charging routine and delays charging past 80% until just before you wake up to preserve battery health.
support: Li-ion batteries degrade faster when held at 100% voltage for long periods. By keeping the battery at 80% during the night and topping it off only when needed, the chemical stress on the cathode is minimized, extending the battery's lifespan.

question: Why is my C-phone getting hot during video calls?
correct_answer: Video calls consume high resources (camera, screen, 5G/WiFi, encoding), causing the processor to generate significant heat.
support: Simultaneous usage of the ISP (Image Signal Processor), modem, and CPU creates a high thermal load. The phone uses the metal frame as a heat sink to dissipate this heat, so it feeling warm is normal. If it becomes too hot to hold, the thermal throttle will dim the screen and lower frame rates.

question: Does the C-phone support eSIM?
correct_answer: Yes, all C-phone models from series 12 onwards support Dual SIM (one physical Nano-SIM and one eSIM) or Dual eSIM.
support: eSIM allows you to download a cellular plan without a physical card. You can store multiple eSIM profiles (e.g., for travel) but only one can be active at a time alongside the physical SIM.

question: How do I transfer data from my old Android phone to a C-phone?
correct_answer: Use the 'C-Switch' app. Install it on both devices, connect via WiFi Direct, and select the data you wish to transfer.
support: C-Switch creates a private high-speed WiFi hotspot between devices. It can transfer contacts, photos, messages, and most apps. Note that app data (like game saves) may not transfer unless backed up to the cloud.

question: Why is Face Unlock failing in low light?
correct_answer: The standard C-phone uses 2D camera recognition which relies on visible light. The Pro model uses IR sensors for low-light unlocking.
support: For non-Pro models, the screen can flash bright white to illuminate your face ('Screen Fill Light'). However, without a Dot Projector or ToF sensor (found in Pro models), the camera cannot see depth in pitch darkness.

question: What is the 'Private Safe' feature?
correct_answer: It is an encrypted folder for files that is protected by a separate password or biometric authentication.
support: Files moved to Private Safe are moved to a sandboxed partition on the storage and encrypted using AES-256. They are inaccessible to other apps and do not appear in the global file manager or gallery.

question: How do I reset my C-phone if the screen is unresponsive?
correct_answer: Force a restart by holding the Power and Volume Down buttons simultaneously for 10-15 seconds.
support: This hardware interrupt cuts power to the mainboard logic momentarily, forcing the bootloader to reload the OS kernel. It is safe and does not delete user data.

question: Why does the C-phone wifi disconnect frequently?
correct_answer: This can be caused by 'WiFi Assistant' switching to mobile data if the signal is weak, or router incompatibility with WiFi 6.
support: Disable 'WiFi Assistant' in settings. If using an older router, try separating the 2.4GHz and 5GHz bands. The C-phone's aggressive power-save mode on the WiFi chip can sometimes drop connection on congested channels.

question: Does the C-phone have a headphone jack?
correct_answer: No, the C-phone series removed the 3.5mm jack to allow for a larger battery and better water resistance. Use USB-C or Bluetooth headphones.
support: The internal volume occupied by the jack mechanism is significant. Removing it allowed for a 15% increase in battery capacity and simplified the IP68 sealing process. A USB-C to 3.5mm DAC adapter is included in the box.

question: What is the refresh rate of the C-phone screen?
correct_answer: The screen supports an adaptive refresh rate from 1Hz to 120Hz (LTPO technology).
support: LTPO (Low-Temperature Polycrystalline Oxide) allows the display controller to dynamically adjust the refresh rate. It drops to 1Hz for static images (saving power) and ramps up to 120Hz for scrolling and gaming (smoothness).

question: How do I take a screenshot on the C-phone?
correct_answer: Press Power and Volume Down quickly, or use the three-finger swipe down gesture.
support: The gesture can be enabled in 'Settings > Gestures'. It uses the touchscreen digitizer's multi-touch input to recognize the specific pattern of three points moving vertically.

question: Why are my photos blurry at night?
correct_answer: Night mode requires the camera to be held still for 2-3 seconds to capture multiple exposures. Motion blur occurs if the hand shakes.
support: Night mode uses computational photography (stacking). It takes several underexposed images and combines them to reduce noise and boost brightness. Any movement during this capture window aligns the frames incorrectly, causing blur.

question: Can I uninstall pre-installed apps on the C-phone?
correct_answer: Most bloatware can be uninstalled. Core system apps can only be disabled, not fully removed, to prevent system instability.
support: 'Disabling' an app removes it from the app drawer and prevents it from running in the background, effectively effectively freeing up RAM, even if the APK remains in the read-only system partition.

question: What is 'Ram Boost' or 'Virtual RAM'?
correct_answer: It reserves a portion of your internal storage (ROM) to use as temporary RAM when physical memory is full.
support: This uses the pagefile concept (SWAP). While it allows more apps to stay open, the internal storage is significantly slower than physical RAM, so switching to these apps may take a split second longer.

question: How do I update the C-phone software?
correct_answer: Go to Settings > System > Software Update. Ensure you have >50% battery or are plugged in.
support: The update package is verified using a cryptographic signature before installation. The phone uses an A/B partition system, installing the update in the background partition and swapping on reboot to minimize downtime.

question: Why is my GPS accuracy low?
correct_answer: Ensure 'High Accuracy' mode is on. Metal cases or tall buildings (multipath interference) can block satellite signals.
support: GPS relies on line-of-sight to at least 4 satellites. 'High Accuracy' uses WiFi and Cell Tower triangulation to assist the satellite lock (A-GPS). If you are indoors or in a dense urban canyon, the signal reflects off buildings, confusing the receiver.

question: Does the C-phone support wireless powershare?
correct_answer: Yes, you can charge other Qi-compatible devices (like earbuds) by placing them on the back of the C-phone. Enable it in the Quick Settings toggle.
support: This feature uses the inductive charging coil in reverse. It is limited to 5W output and automatically turns off if the C-phone battery drops below 30% to preserve your own power.

question: What is the warranty period for the C-phone?
correct_answer: The standard warranty is 12 months for the device and 6 months for the battery/accessories, covering manufacturing defects.
support: Physical damage (cracked screen) and liquid damage are not covered under standard warranty. Company C offers an extended protection plan ('C-Care') that covers accidental damage for a fee.

question: How do I enable Developer Options?
correct_answer: Go to Settings > About Phone > Tap 'Build Number' 7 times. Enter your PIN when prompted.
support: Developer options unlock advanced debugging tools (USB Debugging, animation scales, OEM unlocking). They are hidden to prevent average users from accidentally changing settings that could compromise security or performance.

question: Why does my battery drain fast after an update?
correct_answer: It is normal for battery drain to increase for 1-2 days after an update as the OS re-indexes files and optimizes app caches.
support: Background optimization tasks (compiling ART profiles for apps) run heavily after an update. The system needs time to rebuild usage patterns. If drain persists after 48 hours, a factory reset might be needed to clear conflicting legacy data.

question: What is the 'Reading Mode'?
correct_answer: It filters out blue light and adjusts screen temperature to warmer tones to reduce eye strain, especially at night.
support: Blue light suppresses melatonin production, affecting sleep. Reading mode (often called Night Light) creates a yellow/amber tint. There is also a 'Paper Mode' which adds a grain texture to simulate e-ink.

question: How do I clean the C-phone charging port?
correct_answer: Use a non-conductive pick (wood/plastic) or compressed air. Do not use metal pins which can short the contacts.
support: Lint accumulation in the USB-C port prevents the cable from seating fully, causing slow charging or connection drops. The contacts are delicate gold-plated pins; metal tools can scratch or bend them.

question: Can I use two Bluetooth headphones at once?
correct_answer: Yes, via the 'Dual Audio' feature in Bluetooth settings.
support: This utilizes Bluetooth 5.0+'s increased bandwidth. You can control the volume of each device independently. Note that there might be a slight synchronization delay between the two headsets.

question: What is the aperture of the main camera?
correct_answer: The C-phone main camera has an f/1.8 aperture, allowing good low-light performance.
support: A lower f-number means a wider opening, letting in more light. f/1.8 allows the sensor to capture usable images in dim environments without cranking up the ISO (which adds noise).

question: How do I block a number on C-phone?
correct_answer: Open the Phone app, tap the number in Recents, press the 'i' icon, and select 'Block'.
support: Blocking prevents calls and SMS. The blocked caller will hear a busy signal or go straight to voicemail. Their messages will be silently discarded or moved to a 'Spam and Blocked' folder.

question: Why is 'Ok Google' / 'Hey C-Voice' not working?
correct_answer: Check if 'Voice Match' is retrained. Microphone permissions might be denied for the assistant app.
support: The always-listening low-power core needs a precise voice model. If your voice has changed (cold, background noise) or the model is corrupted, retrain it in Assistant settings.

question: What is the storage type of the C-phone?
correct_answer: It uses UFS 4.0 storage for faster read/write speeds compared to the older UFS 3.1.
support: UFS 4.0 offers speeds up to 4200MB/s. This drastically improves app load times, file transfers, and the smoothness of recording 4K/8K video, which requires high write bandwidth.

question: How do I factory reset the C-phone?
correct_answer: Settings > System > Reset Options > Erase all data (factory reset). Back up data first!
support: This wipes the /data partition, removing all user accounts, apps, and files. It returns the phone to the state it was in when it left the factory (though the OS version remains the latest installed).

question: Why does my C-phone get hot while charging?
correct_answer: Heat is a byproduct of the chemical reaction in the battery and the voltage conversion. Fast charging generates more heat.
support: The charging circuit steps down the voltage from the wall adapter. This conversion is not 100% efficient; the energy loss is heat. The phone slows down charging speed as it gets warmer to protect the battery chemistry.

question: Does the C-phone track my location when GPS is off?
correct_answer: It can still estimate location using cell towers and WiFi networks if 'Google Location Accuracy' is enabled for emergency services.
support: E911 regulations require phones to be locatable. Even with the GPS radio off, the phone scans for visible WiFi SSIDs and Cell IDs, sending this data to a location server to triangulate position.

question: How do I customize the Always On Display (AOD)?
correct_answer: Settings > Personalization > Always On Display. You can choose clocks, text, or images.
support: AOD lights up individual pixels on the OLED screen while keeping black pixels off. Using minimal graphics consumes very little power (~1% per hour), but complex animated AODs will drain more.

question: Why are my calls dropping in specific areas?
correct_answer: It is likely a 'dead zone' for your carrier coverage, or network congestion. Try enabling WiFi Calling.
support: Radio waves are blocked by concrete, metal, and terrain. 'WiFi Calling' routes the voice packets over your home internet instead of the cellular tower, solving indoor coverage issues.

question: What is the SAR value of the C-phone?
correct_answer: The Specific Absorption Rate (SAR) is 0.95 W/kg (Head) and 1.10 W/kg (Body), well within the FCC limit of 1.6 W/kg.
support: SAR measures the rate of RF energy absorption by the body. Company C designs antennas to radiate away from the head where possible. These values are maximums; in normal use, the phone lowers power to the minimum needed for a signal.

question: How do I use split-screen multitasking?
correct_answer: Open Recents, tap the app icon at the top of a card, and select 'Split Top'. Then select the second app.
support: Not all apps support split-screen (e.g., some games). The OS resizes the activities dynamically. You can drag the middle bar to adjust the ratio between the two apps.

question: Why does the screen tint yellow in the evening?
correct_answer: 'Eye Comfort Shield' or 'Night Light' is scheduled to turn on at sunset.
support: This creates a warmer color temperature (lower Kelvin) to reduce blue light exposure before sleep. You can disable the schedule or adjust the intensity in Display settings.

question: Can I replace the battery myself?
correct_answer: The C-phone has a sealed glass back. DIY replacement is difficult and voids warranty. Go to an authorized service center.
support: Opening the device requires a heat gun to soften the adhesive and suction cups. The battery is glued in place. Puncturing the battery during removal is a fire hazard.

question: What is the megapixel count of the selfie camera?
correct_answer: The front camera is 32MP using a Quad-Bayer filter, outputting 8MP images by default for better light sensitivity.
support: High megapixel counts on small sensors result in tiny pixels. Pixel binning (combining 4 pixels into 1) creates a 'super pixel' that captures more light, resulting in a clearer 8MP photo than a noisy 32MP one.

question: How do I turn off 5G to save battery?
correct_answer: Settings > Connections > Mobile Networks > Network Mode. Select 'LTE/3G/2G (Auto Connect)'.
support: 5G modems, especially first-gen or mmWave, consume more power than LTE. If you are in an area with spotty 5G, the phone works harder to maintain the signal. Switching to LTE can extend battery life by 10-20%.

question: Why is the fingerprint sensor slow?
correct_answer: Dirt or water on the finger, or a low-quality screen protector, can interfere with the optical/ultrasonic sensor.
support: Optical sensors take a 2D photo of your print; scratches on a screen protector scatter the light. Ultrasonic sensors (in high-end models) map 3D ridges; thick glass protectors block the sound waves. Re-register your finger after applying a new protector.

question: What comes in the box with the C-phone?
correct_answer: The phone, a USB-C to USB-C cable, a SIM eject tool, and quick start guide. The charging brick and earphones are no longer included.
support: This follows industry trends to reduce e-waste and packaging size (shipping efficiency). Most users already own a compatible USB-C charger.
